A few small peach trees on the west side of the road. Access: Public.

Two large Mexican Plum trees, decent amount of fruit even in September. Located on the sidewalk of Mapleshade, with a slight overhang into the parking lot Access: Private but overhanging. Falling Fruit season: September-November.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.

One lone mexican plum in the park with low to medium blooms of fruit. Kids from the elementary school might have shook them down. Decent yeild. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: September-November.
6575 Shady Oaks Dr, Frisco, TX 75034, USA
Falling Fruit record for American persimmon.

Plums the size of golf balls, dark red, thought maybe Allred or Morris plums. Tart little things with moderate pits, but with the amount of trees and fallen fruit, there is quite a gathering to make several batches of plum jelly, or as I did, ferment to make p

3 mid-size plum trees along the water. Just starting to fruit and should be ready soon. There are also 3 on the other side of the pond. Should yeild great fruit, hopefully we can get to them before the squirrels do!

Almost every tree off the end of this path is a high-yield American Plum. In total around this lake/river and through the surrounding parkland, there have to be 15-20 trees. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-September.
3 fruiting plums flanking the pond. Access: Public.
